concerns.text: Constitution Principle VI requires HumanEval or MBPP for LLM-generated code, but plan/spec uses CodeParrot/CodeGen. This dataset mismatch means the analysis won't validate the research question as defined by the Constitution. Either amend Constitution VI or change spec to use HumanEval/MBPP.

Constitution Principle VII requires TinyLlama-1.1B for metric generation, but plan/spec uses radon/pylint. This tooling deviation means the plan cannot run as written under Constitution constraints. Either amend Constitution VII or change spec to use TinyLlama-1.1B.

Research.md states 'NO verified source found' for CodeSearchNet and CodeParrot/CodeGen. While upstream resolver guarantees URL reachability, the plan should document which HF dataset identifiers (e.g., 'codeparrot/codeparrot-training') will be attempted to enable reproducibility.
